name: Canary Islands (Spain)

colors.symbolism: from the colours of the Province of Santa Cruz de Tenerife
from the colours of the Province of Las Palmas

description: Three equal vertical stripes of white, blue, and yellow from the hoist.
history: Adopted by the Statute of Autonomy of 10 August 1982. The civil version is the plain tricolour shown here; the official state flag adds the Canarian coat of arms (a shield with seven islands flanked by two dogs, beneath a royal crown) to the centre. The colours bring together those of the two historic provinces formed in the 1927 division of the archipelago.

features.symbolism: The flag combines the colours of the maritime ensigns of the two historic provinces of the Canary Islands: white and blue from the Province of Santa Cruz de Tenerife, and blue and yellow from the Province of Las Palmas.
